data.title: Understanding the Cyclical Nature of Bear Markets in the Crypto Industry
data.description: Crypto KOL @roger73005305 posted on X. Many people struggle to understand the necessity of bear markets and significant downturns in the cryptocurrency industry. However, these declines are essential for future growth. Without substantial drops, the next cycle of rapid increases would not occur, and without such surges, the industry would stagnate. This cycle of sharp declines, followed by sharp increases and industry recovery, is continuous, much like the changing seasons. It is an unbreakable cycle where decline paves the way for better growth, reflecting the fundamental nature of all things, including finance.

data.title: China Mandates Testing for Toxin in Infant Formula
data.description: China's market regulator has mandated that infant formula manufacturers test for cereulide, a toxin that has led to precautionary recalls by major food companies globally. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increased scrutiny on food safety in the country. This directive comes amid growing concerns over food safety standards and aims to prevent potential health risks associated with the toxin. The move reflects China's commitment to ensuring the safety and quality of food products, particularly those intended for infants. The regulator's decision underscores the importance of rigorous testing and monitoring in the food industry to safeguard public health.

data.title: Embrace Opportunities When Fortune Strikes, Says Crypto KOL
data.description:
Crypto KOL @roger73005305 posted on X, emphasizing the importance of recognizing and seizing opportunities when fortune favors you. He likened this experience to a strong push from behind, making it difficult to stop once momentum is gained. This concept, he noted, is not mystical but rather a natural progression once a trend begins. 

Drawing from ancient wisdom, he mentioned that those who have been patient and persistent will eventually achieve great distances and heights. During challenging times, he advised against anxiety, suggesting that the universe prepares individuals by allowing them to gather strength. 

He encouraged continued effort, reassuring that what is meant to come will arrive, and perhaps good fortune is already on its way.

data.title: Greater China's Outbound Mergers and Acquisitions Reach $12 Billion in January
data.description: The volume of outbound mergers and acquisitions from Greater China reached nearly $12 billion in January, marking the highest level for the first month of a year since 2017. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant increase in cross-border deals. This surge in activity underscores the region's growing influence in the global market as companies seek to expand their international presence. The data reflects a robust start to the year for Greater China's outbound investment activities, indicating a strong appetite for overseas expansion among Chinese firms.

data.title: Cryptocurrencies Face Pressure Amid Bitcoin Weakness Warning
data.description: Cryptocurrencies continued to face downward pressure in Asia on Friday following a warning from Standard Chartered about potential further weakness in Bitcoin. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns in the market as the largest U.S. crypto exchange reported a loss in the fourth quarter. The warning from Standard Chartered has added to the cautious sentiment among investors, contributing to the ongoing volatility in the cryptocurrency market. The performance of Bitcoin, a key indicator for the broader crypto market, remains a focal point for traders and analysts. The recent developments underscore the challenges facing the cryptocurrency sector as it navigates regulatory scrutiny and market fluctuations.

data.title: Prices of many traditional financial assets fell, with silver and crude oil weakening.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices fell to $4,955.5 per ounce, a daily decrease of 1.62%; silver prices fell to $76.86 per ounce, a daily decrease of 7.89%. Regarding volatility indices, the BVIX (BTC volatility index) is currently at 52.39, down 6.04% on the day; the EVIX (ETH volatility index) is currently at 70.74, down 5.73% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude oil fell 3.28% to $62.88 per barrel; Brent crude oil fell 3.22% to $67.65 per barrel.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Launches $30 Million Sustainability Fund
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation has announced the establishment of a $30 million sustainability fund aimed at supporting green projects within the Ethereum ecosystem. According to Bitalk News, the fund will focus on investing in technologies and teams that contribute to reducing energy consumption and carbon emissions. Analysts suggest that this move reflects Ethereum's growing attention to environmental and sustainability issues globally. Industry experts emphasize that as environmental regulations become increasingly stringent, blockchain projects will likely shift towards more sustainable solutions, marking a key trend for the future. The creation of this fund may attract investors interested in green technology and enhance the industry's overall environmental awareness. In the long term, this initiative is expected to help Ethereum solidify its leadership position in the competitive blockchain market.

data.title: Australia's January Consumer Spending Boosted by Summer Events
data.description: Australia's consumer spending saw an increase in January, driven by tennis and other summer events, according to private data. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while this boost is notable, the anticipation of rising borrowing costs could potentially dampen this momentum in the coming months.

data.title: Key Areas of Focus Highlighted in Recent Reports
data.description: Three significant areas were highlighted in today's reports. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X, emphasizing the importance of these topics. The reports delve into various aspects that are crucial for understanding current market dynamics. While the full details are available to members, the highlighted areas provide a glimpse into the broader analysis offered. These insights are part of an ongoing effort to keep stakeholders informed about relevant developments.

data.title: Top and Worst Performing ETFs of the Week
data.description: Japan $EWJ, Utilities $XLU, and Australia $EWA have emerged as the top-performing key ETFs this week. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. In contrast, Bitcoin $IBIT, natural gas $UNG, and Financials $XLF have been the least successful in terms of performance.

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Responds to Prime Minister's Critique on Past Policies
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister, Goto Katayama, addressed comments made by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi regarding the overly tight policies of the past. According to Jin10, Katayama acknowledged that previous budgets did not account for rising prices and labor costs. This response highlights ongoing discussions within Japan's government about the need to adapt fiscal strategies to current economic conditions.

data.title: Credit Markets Show Resilience Amid Stock Volatility
data.description: Credit markets have demonstrated stability despite significant fluctuations in certain stock prices. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that the trading day revealed minimal concern from credit markets regarding the downturn in some stocks. This observation highlights the resilience of credit markets in the face of stock market volatility. The daily wrap commentary suggests that while some stocks are experiencing declines, the broader credit market remains largely unaffected, indicating a potential divergence in market reactions.

data.title: Trove Markets secretly refunded KOLs after the price of its cryptocurrency plummeted, causing significant losses for ICO participants.
data.description: Bubblemaps disclosed on the X platform that Trove Markets had quietly refunded KOLs who participated in the pre-sale, while other pre-sale participants suffered heavy losses. Monitoring wallets associated with TROVE token deployers revealed that a day after the token crash, $100,000 worth of USDC and $350,000 worth of USDT were transferred to newly invested wallets. On-chain evidence and leaked chat logs now prove that the project team engaged in discriminatory treatment of investors. After raising $11.5 million through an ICO in January, Trove stated it would retain $9,397,403 to continue building PerpDEX on Solana. This move sparked strong community dissatisfaction, causing its token price to plummet by over 95%, resulting in significant losses for ICO participants who accused the team of rigging. According to community feedback, the project had issues such as "paying KOLs for promotion, and funds flowing to entertainment platform deposit addresses."

data.title: Polygon Proposes Measures to Enhance AI Agent Transactions
data.description:
Polygon announced on the X platform that for AI agents to autonomously conduct commercial transactions and payments at scale, friction must be eliminated, and payments should be instantaneous with nearly zero fees. According to Odaily, the PIP-82 proposal suggests reclaiming up to $1 million in base fuel fees through the Polygon x402 facilitator to accelerate transactions between agents and further reduce AI agent costs.

Polygon holds a significant share in Agentic Commerce, having processed several key AI agent transactions. Notably, OpenClaw completed the first fully autonomous AI-to-AI commercial transaction on Polygon, involving automatic listing, pricing, negotiation, transaction, and payment.

data.title: Polygon: Plans to recover $1 million in fuel costs to optimize inter-agent transactions.
data.description: Polygon, in a statement on its X platform, stated that the commercial scaling of intelligent agents (where AI agents autonomously complete commercial transactions and payments) requires the elimination of friction, and payments need to be instantaneous and have predictable near-zero fees. The PIP-82 proposal suggests recovering up to $1 million in fuel costs through the Polygon x402 facilitator program to accelerate transactions between agents and further reduce AI agent costs. Polygon reportedly holds a significant share in Agentic Commerce and has processed several key AI agent transactions. For example, OpenClaw completed its first fully autonomous AI-to-AI commercial transaction on Polygon (automatic listing, pricing, negotiation, closing, and payment collection).

data.title: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has raised $30 billion in Series G funding, led by GIC and others.
data.description: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has raised $30 billion in Series G funding, valuing the company at $380 billion post-money. The round was led by GIC and Coatue, with participation from D.E. Shaw Ventures, Dragoneer, Founders Fund, ICONIQ, and MGX, among others. Accel, Addition, JPMorgan Chase, Sequoia Capital, and Temasek also participated. The funds raised will be used to deepen research, continue product innovation, and ensure the team has sufficient resources to expand its infrastructure.

data.title: Bangladesh's New Leader Faces Diplomatic Balancing Act with India and China
data.description: Bangladesh's newly appointed leader is tasked with navigating diplomatic relations with both India and China, ensuring the country maintains its sovereignty without becoming overly reliant on either nation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by the leader in balancing these crucial ties. As Bangladesh continues to develop economically, the strategic partnerships with these neighboring countries will play a significant role in shaping its future. The leader's ability to manage these relationships effectively will be pivotal in maintaining Bangladesh's independence and fostering growth.

data.title: Shenzhen Financial Authority Issues Warning on Illegal Gold Trading Activities
data.description: Shenzhen's Local Financial Regulatory Bureau has issued a public notice to further regulate gold market operations. According to Jin10, the notice prohibits companies from engaging in illegal gold trading activities such as pre-pricing transactions, leveraged trading, and deferred transactions. These activities often occur through online platforms like WeChat groups, mini-programs, apps, and websites, where customers are allowed to pay a margin to lock in the price of a certain weight of gold. The transactions are then settled based on price fluctuations, allowing for closing positions without actual physical gold delivery. Additionally, the notice warns against illegal fundraising activities disguised as gold custody, leasing, or buyback agreements that promise fixed returns to customers.

data.title: Ho Chi Minh City Addresses Karaoke Noise Concerns Amid Middle-Class Demands
data.description: Ho Chi Minh City is taking measures to address noise pollution caused by karaoke activities, responding to demands from Vietnam's growing middle class for a quieter environment. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the city's efforts to regulate noise levels as residents seek more peace and tranquility. The crackdown comes as the middle class in Vietnam continues to expand, bringing with it increased expectations for improved living conditions and reduced disturbances. Authorities are implementing stricter regulations to ensure that karaoke establishments comply with noise control standards, aiming to balance entertainment with community well-being. The initiative reflects broader societal changes as economic growth leads to shifts in lifestyle preferences and urban development.

data.title: Global Institutions Bullish on Gold Amid Geopolitical Risks
data.description:
On February 13, Jin10 reported that CICC Wealth Management released an analysis of the fundamentals of precious metals, highlighting a strong bullish sentiment towards gold among major global institutions. According to Jin10, the consensus is driven by factors such as the normalization of geopolitical conflict risks, the ongoing global de-dollarization of assets, central banks purchasing gold, geopolitical risks, and a shift in gold pricing from a real interest rate framework to a credit risk hedging framework.

From an investment perspective, a simple calculation suggests that the proportion of investable gold could exceed the 2011 peak of 3.6% between 2026 and 2028, with gold prices potentially rising to $5,100-$6,000 per ounce. Regarding silver, it is believed that after the gold-silver ratio is corrected to a range of 55-80, any excessive increase in silver prices may face policy suppression, reducing the risk of a squeeze. Silver is expected to primarily follow the trend of gold.
